title: Integrating qPCR in Routine Surveillance of Animal-Based Foods
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Animal-based     foods   are   among     the     most     vulnerable     commodities     to     adulteration  ,   contamination  ,   and     mislabeling  .   Traditional     microbiological     and     biochemical     tests     often     require     days     to     generate     results  ,   creating     delays   in   food     safety     interventions  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     highly     specific     detection     of     species     identity  ,   pathogens  ,   and     contaminants     within     hours  .   Integrating     qPCR     into     routine     surveillance     programs     strengthens     traceability  ,   ensures     regulatory     compliance  ,   and     builds     consumer     trust   in   meat  ,   milk  ,   eggs  ,   and     seafood     products  . By   enabling     simultaneous     detection     of     multiple     species     or     contaminants  ,   qPCR     also     improves     laboratory     efficiency     and     reduces     operational     costs   in   the   long   run  . National   adoption     of     qPCR     surveillance     can     significantly     reduce     food     fraud  ,   improve     public     health  ,   and     support   global   trade     competitiveness  .
